q.en: What makes ostriches unusual among birds, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据本书，鸵鸟在鸟类中因什么而与众不同？

options.en: They can fly higher than any other bird.
options.zh: 它们飞得比其他任何鸟都高。

options.en: They live only in the oceans.
options.zh: 它们只生活在海洋里。

options.en: They are the world's largest living birds but cannot fly, and are native only to Africa.
options.zh: 它们是世上现存最大的鸟却不会飞，且仅原产于非洲。

options.en: They are the smallest birds and live in trees. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它们是最小的鸟，住在树上。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says 'Ostriches are the world's largest living birds. Even though they can't fly ... they are still birds' and 'are only native to Africa.'
explain.zh: 书中说'鸵鸟是现存最大的鸟。尽管不会飞……它们仍是鸟'，且'仅原产于非洲'。

q.en: How many toes does an ostrich have, and how fast can it sprint?
q.zh: 鸵鸟有几根脚趾，能跑多快？

options.en: One toe, and it hops slowly.
options.zh: 一根脚趾，慢慢跳。

options.en: Four toes, and it can run 10 mph.
options.zh: 四根脚趾，时速10英里。

options.en: Three toes, and it can sprint up to 100 mph. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 三根脚趾，冲刺时速达100英里。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Two toes, and it can sprint up to 45 miles per hour (72.4 kph).
options.zh: 两根脚趾，冲刺速度可达每小时45英里（72.4公里）。

explain.en: Unlike most birds' four toes, 'the ostrich ... has only two' and 'can sprint up to 45 miles per hour.'
explain.zh: 与多数鸟的四趾不同，'鸵鸟……只有两根脚趾'，且'冲刺可达每小时45英里'。

q.en: According to the book, which animal are ostriches among the closest living relatives of?
q.zh: 根据本书，鸵鸟是哪种动物现存的近亲之一？

options.en: The penguin.
options.zh: 企鹅。

options.en: The blue whale. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 蓝鲸。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The house cat.
options.zh: 家猫。

options.en: Tyrannosaurus rex (T. rex).
options.zh: 霸王龙（T. rex）。

explain.en: The book states, 'Ostriches are among the closest living relatives of T. rex' and scientists compared their leg anatomy to T. rex.
explain.zh: 书中说'鸵鸟是霸王龙现存的近亲之一'，科学家还把鸵鸟腿部结构与霸王龙作比较。

q.en: What is special about ostrich eggs?
q.zh: 鸵鸟蛋有什么特别之处？

options.en: They are the smallest eggs in the world.
options.zh: 它们是世界上最小的蛋。

options.en: They are the largest of any bird, weighing about 3 pounds (1.4 kg) each.
options.zh: 它们是所有鸟类中最大的蛋，每个约重3磅（1.4公斤）。

options.en: They are brightly colored like rainbows. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们像彩虹一样色彩鲜艳。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They can float on water.
options.zh: 它们能浮在水面上。

explain.en: The dominant female's eggs 'are the largest of any bird, weighing about 3 pounds each (1.4 kg).'
explain.zh: 优势雌鸟的蛋'是所有鸟中最大的，每个约重3磅（1.4公斤）'。

options.en: To make their voices louder. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了让叫声更响亮。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because they have no teeth, stones in the gizzard help grind up their food.
options.zh: 因为它们没有牙齿，砂囊里的石头能帮助磨碎食物。

options.en: To build their nests.
options.zh: 为了筑巢。

options.en: To help them fly better.
options.zh: 为了飞得更好。

explain.en: Since ostriches are toothless, 'they need to eat stones to aid their digestion' — about 2.2 pounds in the gizzard grind food.
explain.zh: 鸵鸟没有牙，'需要吞石头助消化'——砂囊里约2.2磅石头把食物磨碎。

q.en: How does the book describe an ostrich's eyes?
q.zh: 本书如何描述鸵鸟的眼睛？

options.en: They are the biggest of any land animal, about 2 inches (5 cm) across.
options.zh: 它们是陆地上动物中最大的眼睛，直径约2英寸（5厘米）。

options.en: They are the smallest eyes of any bird. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们是所有鸟中最小的眼睛。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They are hidden under feathers.
options.zh: 它们藏在羽毛下。

options.en: They can see only in black and white.
options.zh: 它们只能看黑白。

explain.en: An ostrich's eyes 'are the biggest of any animal on land. They measure 2 inches (5 cm) across.'
explain.zh: 鸵鸟的眼睛'是陆地上动物中最大的，直径约2英寸（5厘米）'。

q.en: How do ostriches usually live, and who leads the group?
q.zh: 鸵鸟通常怎样生活，由谁领导群体？

options.en: In trees, led by monkeys.
options.zh: 在树上，由猴子领导。

options.en: In herds led by a dominant female only. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 由唯一的优势雌鸟领导的群体。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: In herds of five to fifty, with one dominant adult male (the rooster) in charge.
options.zh: 成群生活，5到50只，由一只占优势的公鸟（公鸡）领导。

options.en: Always completely alone, with no group.
options.zh: 总是一个人，没有群体。

explain.en: Ostrich herds 'usually consist of one dominant adult male (the rooster), several females (the hens), and ostrich chicks.'
explain.zh: 鸵鸟群'通常由一只占优势的公鸟（公鸡）、几只雌鸟（母鸡）和雏鸟组成'。

q.en: Where do ostriches live in the wild today?
q.zh: 如今野生的鸵鸟生活在哪里？

options.en: On the eastern and southern plains (savanna) of the African continent.
options.zh: 在非洲大陆东部和南部的平原（热带草原）上。

options.en: In the mountains of Europe only.
options.zh: 只在欧洲的山区。

options.en: In the forests of South America. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 在南美洲的森林里。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: On the frozen ice of Antarctica.
options.zh: 在南极的冰面上。

explain.en: The book says they live 'in the eastern and southern plains of the African continent' on the dry savanna.
explain.zh: 书中说它们生活在'非洲大陆东部和南部的平原'上干旱的草原。

q.en: In this book, what is a 'savanna'?
q.zh: 在这本书中，'savanna'（热带草原）是什么？

options.en: A deep ocean trench.
options.zh: 深海沟。

options.en: A flat grassland with few trees.
options.zh: 一片树木稀少的平坦草原。

options.en: A kind of bird nest.
options.zh: 一种鸟巢。

options.en: A type of ostrich feather. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种鸵鸟羽毛。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'savanna' as 'a flat grassland with few trees.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 savanna 定义为'一片树木稀少的平坦草原'。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about ostrich characteristics and how they survive in the harsh savanna.
options.zh: 向读者介绍鸵鸟的特征，以及它们如何在严酷草原中生存。

options.en: To convince readers to keep ostriches as pets.
options.zh: 劝说读者把鸵鸟当宠物养。

options.en: To teach readers how to cook ostrich meat.
options.zh: 教读者如何烹饪鸵鸟肉。

options.en: To tell a fictional story about a talking ostrich. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 讲一个会说话的鸵鸟的虚构故事。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The focus question asks 'What characteristics enable ostriches to survive in the harsh conditions of the African savanna?' — the book is informational.
explain.zh: 焦点问题问'鸵鸟的哪些特征使它们能在非洲草原的严酷环境中生存？'——属于知识性内容。

prompt.en: Ostriches cannot fly, yet they survive on the open savanna alongside fast predators like lions and cheetahs. Which of their adaptations do you think is most important — their speed, their sharp eyesight, or their powerful kicking legs — and why?
prompt.zh: 鸵鸟不会飞，却能在有狮子、猎豹等快速捕食者的开阔草原上生存。你认为它们的哪种适应最重要——速度、锐利的视力，还是有力的踢腿？为什么？

en: Recall that ostriches sprint up to 45 mph, have the biggest eyes of any land animal, and can deliver a powerful kick with clawed feet.
Think about how their long neck and large eyes help them 'spot danger from a great distance.'
zh: 回想鸵鸟冲刺可达45英里/时，拥有陆地上最大的眼睛，还能用带爪的脚猛烈踢击。
想想它们长脖子和大眼睛如何帮助'从很远的地方发现危险'。
